title: Hedge
author: Jane Delury
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Hedge" book by Jane Delury?
Options:
A. You cant judge a book by its cover, Louise singsonged and waved her bouquet. I know that, Ella said. Its justhe kind of freaks me out. The way he looks without looking. I see what you mean, Maud said. But hes nice. I promise. Hes just different. Chris had looked at Ella the same way he looked at Louise, which was the same way that he sometimes looked at Maud or Gabriel or his father. There was nothing sexual to the gaze. But maybe it was enough to be stared at by a man for Ella to feel uncomfortable. As they took the shortcut through the orchard, Maud remembered visiting a public pool with her friend when she was eleven.
B. Louise recited the old adage about not judging things by appearances, swinging her bouquet playfully as she spoke. Ella acknowledged the wisdom of that saying, but admitted feeling unsettled by Chris's intense, penetrating gaze that seemed to look right through people. Maud tried to reassure Ella that Chris was a nice person despite his unusual manner of staring at everyone in the same intense way, whether it was her, Louise, Gabriel, or even his own father. His look wasn't sexual, just different. Still, Ella felt uncomfortable being examined so closely by a man. As the group walked through the orchard, Maud recalled a visit to a public pool with a friend when she was 11 years old.
C. Louise said you can't judge a book by its cover while waving her bouquet around. Ella agreed with the saying but said Chris's stare creeped her out, the way he looked without really looking. Maud said she understood but that Chris was nice, just different. His gaze at Ella was the same as at Louise, Maud, Gabriel, or his dad - not sexual, just intense. But that was enough to make Ella uneasy being stared at like that by a man. As they took a shortcut through the orchard, Maud remembered going to a public pool with a friend when she was 11.
D. Louise recited the old saying about not judging by appearances, waving her bouquet playfully. Ella agreed, but said Chris's stare made her uncomfortable - the way he looked without really looking. Maud said she understood, but promised Chris was nice, just different. His gaze at Ella was no different than at Louise, Maud, Gabriel or his dad - not sexual, just intense. But being stared at like that by a man was enough to unsettle Ella. As they walked through the orchard, Maud recalled visiting a public pool with a friend when she was 11.
Answer:

But maybe it was enough to be stared at by a man for Ella to feel uncomfortable. As they took the shortcut through the orchard, Maud remembered visiting a public pool with her friend when she was eleven.
